+pyramid

This item is called **Origin Of Time**, it provides two main benefits:

**Full Refund**: Recovers all XP and materials you invested in the character you use it on.

**Duplicate Conversion**: Only for Standard Awakeners (not for Limited in the Standard pool or the starting SR Awakeners), converts all additional copies into Standard "selectors," which can subsequently be used to obtain or duplicate other Standard Awakeners of your choice (not Limited in Standard).

This process is also applicable to **Standard Wheels** (except the Limited in the Standard pool).

It is primarily obtained from the developers through special in-game mail during specific events, such as anniversaries or major updates. It is used for re-allocating resources to build a better team.

hmmm imo morimens is more akin to Arknights or even FGO in term of unit flexibility, the only limit is actually your own skill

Idk if u play it but I get this idea from HSR where a premium team referring to a Best team composition of a certain character 


Lets take an example 
Bron999 have her premium team that work the best for her so she can achieve her full potential
oh hsr, that hypermeta game
I know i heard some think kinda the same like that like mono tawil team(tawi,ryker,hamel and Gramona/karen)

Skill and experience very required. But depending on which level of endgame content you would like to face, skill and experience could be not enough. You can try how much you want but a lv60 E0 Kath will probably not be enough to clear an Alert 5 in Dzone. I mean, skill is skill. Skill will help you to pilot an adequate team tho

In this game there is a certain level of flexibility indeed. Yet, some characters are GREATLY helped in their potential if you play them with other specific characters or features (Posses or specific covenants or Wheels).
Examples could be Vortice with her Posse or Ghelot which GREATLY enjoy (pretty much requires) Lone Seed posse and Thais (another Awakener) in the team
So we can say there are best in slots for sure
Like in almost every gacha tbh

Basically, and in this order..
1) have Weaver stacks
2) activate annihilation or be in ultra turn
3) activate Arachne Exalt 
4) play cards of other awakeners
It seems complicated but it's not tbh
Every pursuit uses 1 Weaver stack
You can gain more in the same turn you use them of course (always by playing the 0 cost Arachne card)

well considering that im here and accidentally sent a message i might as well ask: is there a benefit for having a 4 chaos team or do they always want to be buddies with another

4 chaos teammates give additional end of turn keyflare and give access to unforgoten twilight

basically if you posse twice a turn you normally pick between 3 random unlocked posses and using one, unforgoten lets you pick *any* posse of your choice and it actives twice, but in exchange can’t be selected again for the rest of the level
thats a little niche but...i kinda like it, ty!!

its pretty versatile and powerful
just needs an invested account since u need all the posses to maximize it
